lorehq

─[ Bash macOS Compatibility ]─ ┐

name: bash-macos-compat description: Avoid Bash 4+ features that break on macOS stock Bash 3.2 trigger: writing bash scripts that will run cross-platform user-invocable: false

Bash macOS Compatibility

macOS ships Bash 3.2 (2007). These features require Bash 4+ and will break:

Broken on Bash 3.2

FeatureBash 4+Portable Alternative
Associative arraysdeclare -A mapPipe-delimited string + case pattern match
Lowercase transform${var,,}echo "$var" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'
Key existence test[[ -v arr[key] ]]case "$str" in *"|${key}|"*) ... ;; esac
readarray / mapfilereadarray -t arrwhile IFS= read -r line; do arr+=("$line"); done
&>> redirectcmd &>> filecmd >> file 2>&1
`&` pipe stderr`cmd

Pattern: Associative Array Replacement

Instead of:

declare -A existing
existing["${key,,}"]=1
[[ -v existing["$lookup"] ]] && echo "found"

Use parallel arrays or delimited strings:

to_lower() { echo "$1" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'; }

existing="|"
existing="${existing}$(to_lower "$key")|"

case "$existing" in
  *"|$(to_lower "$lookup")|"*) echo "found" ;;
esac

Empty Array Expansion with set -u

Bash 3.2 treats "${array[@]}" as an unbound variable error when the array is empty and set -u (nounset) is active.

set -euo pipefail
local items=()
# FAILS on Bash 3.2: items[@]: unbound variable
for item in "${items[@]}"; do echo "$item"; done

# FIX: conditional expansion
for item in ${items[@]+"${items[@]}"}; do echo "$item"; done

The ${var+expr} syntax expands to expr only if var is set, avoiding the unbound error.

Detection

Add #!/usr/bin/env bash (not #!/bin/bash) and comment Bash 3.2+ compat at the top. grep for declare -A, ${.*,,}, ${.*^^}, [[ -v, readarray, mapfile before shipping.

PgUp/PgDn: scroll b: bulk commands q: quit x: menu ← → ↑ ↓: navigate
Donate 0.24.4
